item.title: The Ronettes
item.description: Genres: Girl group, Brill Building, Pop soul, Rhythm & blues Era: 1961–1967 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Ronettes are the girl group at its most potent, the act that proved what happened when Brill Building craft and Phil Spector’s Wall of Sound met a voice strong enough to ride them both.

item.title: (1960) "Will You Love Me Tomorrow"

item.title: (1964) "You Really Got Me"
item.description: The KinksReleasedAugust 1964LabelPye RecordsProducerShel TalmySongwritersRay DaviesGenresPrimaryGarage rockProto-punkSecondaryBritish rhythm & bluesHard rockPower popFreakbeatFrat rockListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Two power chords a whole step apart, hammered through a sound nobody had heard on ...

item.title: Acoustic Chicago blues
item.description: Periodc. 1928–1950LocationChicagoInfluencesCountry bluesRagtimeHokumLinksRYM A guitar that swings instead of moans.

item.title: Electric Texas blues
item.description: Period1942–presentLocationDallas and Houston, later AustinInfluencesAcoustic Texas bluesJazzJump bluesLinksRYM A single-string line that phrases like a horn.
